name: VIABLE Credentials System
observations: Student Verifiable Credentials system built with Flask backend, iOS mobile app, and web frontend
Uses BBS+ cryptographic signatures for privacy-preserving credentials
Supports ngrok tunneling for external access during development
Has issuer, verifier, and settings components
Recently underwent massive cleanup reducing 60+ markdown files to 2 and 68 test files to 28
Located at /Users/patrickherbke/Documents/stvc/
Uses Python 3.9 with virtual environments (venv and test_env)
Runs on HTTPS localhost:8080 with Flask development server

name: Backend Structure
observations: Flask application in backend/ directory with src/ containing main code
Main entry point: backend/main.py
Blueprint-based architecture with issuer/, verifier/, settings/, validate/, usecases/ modules
Uses SQLite database with SQLAlchemy ORM
BBS+ core integration for cryptographic operations
Socket.IO for real-time communication
Static files served from src/static/ (CSS, JS, images)
Jinja2 templates in src/templates/ with component-based structure
Supports multi-tenant settings with TenantSettings model

name: Mobile iOS App
observations: Swift iOS application in mobile/ios/StudentWallet/
Xcode project with SwiftUI interface
Integrates BBS+ core through Rust FFI bindings
Supports credential issuance and presentation
Uses QR code scanning for credential exchange
Keychain integration for secure storage
Domain models for OpenID credentials, DID documents, and presentations
Located in mobile/ios/StudentWallet/StudentWallet/

name: BBS Core Integration
observations: Rust-based BBS+ signature implementation in bbs-core/
Python bindings in backend/bbs-core/python/bbs_core.py
iOS Swift bindings through UniFFI in mobile/ios/bbs-core/
Supports selective disclosure of credential fields
Zero-knowledge proof capabilities
Cross-platform compilation for iOS and Python

name: Testing Infrastructure
observations: Organized test suite in tests/ directory with 28 essential tests
Backend tests in tests/backend/ split by functionality (issuer/, verifier/, settings/, vcstatus/)
Integration tests in tests/integration/ for end-to-end scenarios
Test fixtures in tests/fixtures/
Pytest configuration in tests/conftest.py
iOS tests in mobile/ios/StudentWallet/StudentWalletTests/
API integration tests for all endpoints

name: Network Configuration
observations: Supports both local IP and ngrok tunneling for external access
Real-time ngrok URL updates without server restart implemented
Network settings stored in database with TenantSettings
API endpoints for network configuration at /settings/api/network
Dual URL system: external_server_url for QR codes, local_server_url for Socket.IO
CORS handling for cross-origin requests
HTTPS support with adhoc certificates for development

name: Directory Structure
observations: Professional organization: docs/{development,api,deployment}, tests/{backend,integration,fixtures}, scripts/{development,deployment}
Archives in archives/{backups,deprecated,logs} for old files
Configuration in config/{environments,docker}
Mobile apps in mobile/ directory
Frontend placeholder in frontend/
Migration scripts in migrations/ with Alembic
Instance data in instance/ directory
Build artifacts in target/ directories

name: Documentation System
observations: Comprehensive API documentation in docs/api/ with diagrams
Development guides in docs/development/
Deployment documentation in docs/deployment/
README files at multiple levels for different components
Visual diagrams for BBS protocol and issuance flow
Cleanup and migration documentation preserved
Troubleshooting guides for common issues

name: Build and Deployment
observations: Makefile with commands: make dev, make setup, make test
Docker support with docker-compose.prod.yml for production
Environment-specific configs: .env.development, .env.production, .env.testing
Comprehensive .gitignore covering Python, virtual environments, Flask, databases, logs, IDE files, OS files, Node.js, Rust, iOS
.dockerignore for production builds
Virtual environment management with both venv/ and test_env/
Automated port management and process cleanup

name: UI and Frontend
observations: Tailwind CSS for styling with custom components
Responsive design for mobile and desktop
Component-based template structure in src/templates/components/
JavaScript modules for verifier, settings, and issuer functionality
QR code generation and scanning capabilities
Real-time updates via Socket.IO for verification flows
Modern UI with viable-credentials branding and logo integration
Form handling for credential issuance and settings
